name: IMRC
mission: IMRC has been working for over four decades to support Muslims and minorities in India. We fund education, healthcare, water access, economic programs, and emergency relief.
theoryOfChange: IMRC states it works year round to assess needs of Muslims and minorities in poverty-struck areas of India and develop projects based on their needs, funding education, healthcare, water access, economic programs, and emergency relief through 100+ partners on the ground to create real, measurable change.
scoreSummary: Indian Muslim Relief and Charities shows good alignment and good impact, with zakat compliance (minor risk deductions applied).
zakatClaimEvidence: Dedicated zakat page found at https://www.imrcusa.org/zakat (Source: https://www.imrcusa.org/zakat)

ui_signals_v1.recommendation_rationale: Good donor alignment with manageable risk. Evidence quality is solid but still evolving in places.

keyConcerns.headline: Zakat-collecting organization holds 80 months of reserves
keyConcerns.detail: Zakat must be distributed promptly per Islamic jurisprudence. Large reserves raise concerns about timely distribution.

components.evidence: Explicit zakat program (+4); Muslim-focused organization (+2); Islamic identity (+1); Asnaf: fuqara (+5); Strong humanitarian service (+4)
components.name: Muslim Donor Fit

components.improvement_suggestion: Expand services to underserved populations or geographies with limited nonprofit coverage.
components.name: Underserved Space

alignment.rationale: Alignment 34/50: HIGH Muslim donor fit, advocacy cause area

components.improvement_suggestion: Publish a time-bound plan to deploy excess unrestricted reserves into mission delivery, while keeping a clear operating reserve floor.
components.name: Financial Health

judge_issues.message: The risk summary mentions 80 months of reserves, while the 'Financial Health' component in 'score_details.impact' mentions 55.7 months. This discrepancy should be clarified.
Website shows related EIN (94-2784165), likely parent/subsidiary of 27-0058132

risks.risk_summary: Key concern: Zakat-collecting organization holds 80 months of reserves — zakat must be distributed promptly

risks.description: Zakat-collecting organization holds 80 months of reserves — zakat must be distributed promptly

score_details.score_summary: Indian Muslim Relief and Charities shows good alignment and good impact, with zakat compliance (minor risk deductions applied).

zakat.claim_evidence: Dedicated zakat page found at https://www.imrcusa.org/zakat (Source: https://www.imrcusa.org/zakat)

all_citations.claim: IMRC reported total revenue of $10,004,213, a program expense ratio of 89.8%, and 55.7 months of working capital.
The organization is Zakat-eligible and follows specific policies for asnaf categories.
Financial profile metadata confirms the organization's fiscal reporting and efficiency metrics.
IMRC provides meals, water wells, and medical treatment to beneficiaries in India.
The organization distributed grain meals and supported students and patients.
IMRC continued its mission by supporting students and providing healthcare during the reporting period.
Historical data shows a consistent track record of distributing meals and supporting healthcare.
The charity has long-standing programs for Ramadan/Qurbani beneficiaries and student support.
IMRC offers multiple donation methods for supporters to contribute to their mission.

baseline_narrative.amal_score_rationale: IMRC earns a solid 64/100 score due to its exceptional financial efficiency and strong alignment with the needs of Indian minority communities, though its overall impact score is moderated by the direct service nature of its work.
areas_for_improvement: Enhance data transparency to improve medium-level data confidence scores
Increase third-party impact verification to validate long-term program outcomes

dimension_explanations.alignment: IMRC shows high alignment for Muslim donors through its Zakat-eligible status and focus on urgent advocacy for minority rights in India [2]. The charity has a long track record of service, consistently delivering meals, water wells, and educational support to those in need [4][5][7].
dimension_explanations.impact: The organization demonstrates high financial health with a total revenue of $10,004,213 and a program expense ratio of 89.8% [1][3]. IMRC provides direct services including healthcare treatments, student support, and food distribution, maintaining a significant reserve of 55.7 months of working capital to ensure operational continuity [1][6][8].

baseline_narrative.headline: Indian Muslim Relief and Charities provides essential humanitarian aid and advocacy to empower India's marginalized Muslim and minority communities.
strengths: High program expense ratio of 89.8%, ensuring the vast majority of funds reach beneficiaries
Exceptional fundraising efficiency with a cost of only $0.02 per $1 raised
Robust financial stability with 55.7 months of working capital
baseline_narrative.summary: Indian Muslim Relief and Charities (IMRC) is a non-profit organization dedicated to helping India's Muslims and minorities achieve security, freedom, and equality through comprehensive social programs [1][4]. The organization is Zakat-eligible, allowing donors to fulfill their religious obligations through various asnaf categories [2][9].

all_citations.quote: Zakat Categories: fuqara, masakin, amil, muallaf, riqab, gharimin, fisabilillah, ibn_sabil
Number of voting members of the governing body: 14
Improvement: Publish a time-bound plan to deploy excess unrestricted reserves
Increase third-party impact verification to validate long-term program outcomes
Improvement: Expand services to underserved populations or geographies

rich_narrative.amal_score_rationale: IMRC earns a solid 64/100 score due to its exceptional financial efficiency and strong alignment with the needs of Indian minority communities, though its overall impact score is moderated by the direct service nature of its work.

areas_for_improvement.context: The charity holds <cite id="9">55.7 months of working capital</cite> in reserve; for a zakat-collecting entity, this raises concerns regarding the prompt distribution of funds to those in need.
The organization has experienced a <cite id="17">3-year revenue CAGR of -10.2%</cite>, suggesting a need to revitalize donor engagement to maintain its historical scale of impact.

bbb_assessment.summary: Financial statements are audited, but a full BBB Wise Giving Alliance review is not currently available.

case_against.mitigation_notes: The organization's long history and direct delivery model mitigate risks of fund mismanagement, though donors should seek clarity on reserve deployment.
risk_factors: <cite id="9">Capital efficiency risk due to high reserves</cite>
case_against.summary: The primary concern is the accumulation of <cite id="9">excessive financial reserves</cite> (over 5 years of operating capital), which may conflict with the preference for rapid distribution of zakat.

confidence.data_freshness: Financial data from 2023; Impact data from 2021
confidence.notes: Strong historical data but declining revenue trend requires monitoring.

alignment.explanation: IMRC offers <cite id="10">verified zakat eligibility</cite> and serves <cite id="11">eight asnaf categories</cite>, making it a high-fit option for Muslim donors focused on the Indian subcontinent.
alignment.improvement: The organization could improve alignment by <cite id="22">expanding services to underserved geographies</cite> within India that currently have limited nonprofit coverage.

credibility.explanation: IMRC is a highly established institution with <cite id="16">100% independent board leadership</cite> and a <cite id="1">45-year history</cite> of serving India's minorities. They demonstrate transparency by publishing <cite id="6">detailed annual reports</cite> with specific beneficiary metrics.
credibility.improvement: To strengthen credibility, the charity should <cite id="18">publish a time-bound plan</cite> to deploy its excess unrestricted reserves into mission delivery.

impact.explanation: The organization utilizes a <cite id="19">direct service delivery model</cite>, ensuring that aid like <cite id="13">water wells</cite> and <cite id="7">medical treatments</cite> reaches beneficiaries without excessive intermediaries. However, the <cite id="20">cost per beneficiary is approximately $789.88</cite>, which is average for the humanitarian sector.
impact.improvement: Impact measurement would benefit from <cite id="21">tracking long-term outcomes</cite> beyond immediate outputs like meal counts to demonstrate lasting poverty alleviation.

donor_fit_matrix.evidence_rigor: Grade C - Verified outputs and documented theory of change

grantmaking_profile.grant_strategy: Direct implementation through local staff and infrastructure

rich_narrative.headline: Indian Muslim Relief and Charities provides essential humanitarian aid and advocacy to empower India's marginalized Muslim and minority communities.

ideal_donor_profile.best_for_summary: Best for donors who want to support long-term Islamic institutional presence in India through a charity with extremely low overhead and high financial stability.
donor_motivations: Want to fulfill zakat obligations for the marginalized in India
Value organizations with a 40+ year track record
Prioritize low fundraising costs to ensure more money reaches the field
Care about multi-sectoral aid (water, food, health, and education)
giving_considerations: The organization holds very high reserves, meaning your gift may contribute to long-term stability rather than immediate emergency needs
Revenue has been declining recently, making new donations critical for maintaining scale
Focus is exclusively on India and its minority communities
ideal_donor_profile.not_ideal_for: Donors seeking high-growth 'startup' charities or those who prefer organizations that spend down their entire budget annually.

impact_evidence.evidence_grade_explanation: Grade C reflects verified output tracking (millions of meals and thousands of patients) and a documented theory of change, but lacks independent RCT-level evaluations.
impact_evidence.theory_of_change_summary: IMRC aims to alleviate poverty by providing immediate relief (food, water) while building long-term capacity through education and healthcare to ensure India's minorities achieve equality.
impact_evidence.why_evidence_matters: Evidence ensures that large-scale distributions of food and medical care are reaching the intended populations effectively.

long_term_outlook.room_for_funding_explanation: Despite high reserves, the massive scale of poverty in India and the charity's declining revenue trend create a significant need for new funding to sustain operations.

peer_comparison.differentiator: Distinguished by its <cite id="1">founding in 1981</cite> and its <cite id="5">$0.01 fundraising cost</cite>, which is significantly lower than many peers.

similar_organizations.differentiator: Focuses on US-based advocacy and legal rights
similar_organizations.name: COUNCIL ON AMERICAN-ISLAMIC RELATIONS CALIFORNIA

similar_organizations.differentiator: Focuses on domestic US hunger relief
similar_organizations.name: Feeding America

similar_organizations.differentiator: Focuses on Palestinian humanitarian relief at a smaller scale
similar_organizations.name: Rebuilding Alliance

strengths.detail: The organization spends only <cite id="5">$0.01 to raise every $1</cite> in donations, ensuring that donor acquisition costs do not erode the impact of contributions.
Operating since <cite id="1">1981</cite>, IMRC maintains <cite id="9">55.7 months of working capital</cite>, indicating extreme financial resilience and long-term sustainability.
IMRC delivers massive-scale aid, including <cite id="12">16.4 million meals provided</cite> in 2020 and <cite id="13">809 water wells built</cite> to improve rural infrastructure.

strengths_deep_dive: IMRC's strength lies in its ability to scale direct services across the vast geography of India. Their healthcare program is particularly robust, having <cite id="14">treated 102,314 patients in 2019</cite> and increasing that capacity to <cite id="7">183,100 patients by 2021</cite>. This growth is supported by a <cite id="15">14-member board of directors</cite> providing oversight. Furthermore, the organization maintains <cite id="16">100% independent board members</cite>, which is a critical indicator of strong governance and objective financial management.
rich_narrative.summary: Indian Muslim Relief and Charities (IMRC) is a long-standing humanitarian organization dedicated to supporting India's marginalized Muslim and minority populations. <cite id="1">founded in 1981</cite>, the organization has spent over four decades addressing systemic poverty and inequality through a multi-sectoral approach. In 2023, the organization reported <cite id="2">total revenue of $13,833,809</cite>, primarily driven by <cite id="3">$13.3 million in public contributions</cite>. IMRC operates with a high degree of financial efficiency, maintaining a <cite id="4">program expense ratio of 89.8%</cite> while achieving <cite id="5">fundraising efficiency of $0.02 per $1 raised</cite>. Their work spans critical areas including education, healthcare, and emergency relief. For instance, in 2021 alone, the organization <cite id="6">provided 7.2 million meals</cite> and <cite id="7">treated 183,100 patients</cite> through its medical initiatives. The charity also demonstrates significant long-term stability, holding <cite id="8">$48.7 million in total assets</cite> with <cite id="9">55.7 months of working capital</cite> in reserve. As a <cite id="10">zakat-eligible organization</cite>, IMRC provides a verified pathway for Muslim donors to fulfill their religious obligations across <cite id="11">eight distinct asnaf categories</cite>, including the poor (fuqara) and needy (masakin).
